summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 11 (KRYS-MBUU)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Signed (May-21) definitive sale of Lee to Authentic Brands for up to $1B, Lee in discontinued ops, 2H26 regulatory close, paired with $750M buyback — dated portfolio reshape + capital return.
Live merger-arb: Kimberly-Clark cash-and-stock acquisition, both shareholder bases approved Jan-29, US antitrust cleared, other regulatory pending, 2H26 close; KVUE ~$17.71.
Definitive go-private (Feb-16): Fairfax/CEO-led consortium at $10.90 cash, dual minority-vote thresholds, Q2 2026 close; shares ~$11.01 (above deal -> market sniffing a possible bump/squeeze).
Charter all-stock buyout (0.236x), holders approved, accelerated to close with Charter-Cox; but GCI spin gating and ~June-2027 close keep it slow merger-arb, not imminent.
Ziggo Group (Telenet + bought-in VodafoneZiggo) creation closing 2H-2026, then 90% spin to holders + Euronext listing 2027; classic Liberty SOTP value-unlock with dated milestones.

WHP Global tender (SC TO/14D9 filed) buying up to $100M of stock at substantial premium for 7% stake, closing 1H-2026; dated partial-liquidity event but full sale process concluded (capped, not a takeout).
All-stock merger with Somnigroup, guidance withdrawn, close by end-2026 (LEG holders ~9% of combo) atop $150-200M restructuring; dated arb but stub-equity exchange, not a cash takeout.
$500M Series A pref special-div (record 6/1, pay 6/16 — largely priced) + pending Millicom/Costa Rica SUTEL merger-block appeal still forward.
Live strategic review incl. potential full-company SALE + active Specialty-segment sale process (BofA/Wachtell engaged), activist push; milestones approaching (lead-plaintiff 6/22), no deal yet.
Live sale process: PE-backed Curium bid valuing LNTH ~$7B, board evaluating, decision reportedly weeks away; PYLARIFY franchise could draw competing bidders. Forward merger-arb at $101.

IM-3 lunar landing scheduled H2 2026 — a real dated execution binary (high stakes after IM-2 tipped landing) plus first NSNS data-relay sat, but outcome is operational/sentiment-driven, not a structural value unlock.

Two activists: Jana settled (4 board seats, possible sale of the $7.6bn co) and Starboard now pushing to double cost cuts + sell Asia assets — hardening structural pressure with strategic-review/divestiture optionality live.

Board formally initiated strategic-alternatives review (sale/merger/asset sale) with Morgan Stanley as advisor + Starboard stake — a live, real M&A/sale-process catalyst with the CCS El Dorado consolidation as a sweetener.

Kabanga nickel Final Investment Decision targeted in 2026 (feasibility filed, 100% ownership consolidated from BHP) — a dated value-unlock milestone on a high-grade battery-metals + Hydromet timing moat.
Path Forward Plan: ~$2B asset sales + loan resolutions, ~$1.4-1.5B done, deleveraging into 2H26; real but multi-year operational, not a hard near-term trigger.

Glazers publicly open to selling 51% control (news Jun-4-2026); $33/sh floor must be matched for Ratcliffe pre-Feb-2027 and drag-along arms after, framing dated control-sale optionality with downside support.

Non-healthcare consumer (audio) business held-for-sale/discontinued ops with active ~$850-950M offers; Politan-driven separation clears the overhang on core healthcare co, though closing timeline still soft.
Barington activist board agreement + ongoing Industrial Technologies sale process (JPM) with portfolio reshaped via SGK and $230M warehouse-automation sale; structural value-unlock in motion.
Definitive sale of National Public Finance to Assured Guaranty + board-declared $14/sh special dividend contingent on close & regulatory approval (~Mar-2026); textbook capital-return/wind-down arb.

verified.catalyst: Kontoor signed a definitive agreement (May 21, 2026) to sell the Lee business to Authentic Brands Group for up to $1B ($750M cash + $250M earnout), with proceeds funding a $750M buyback authorization plus term-loan paydown, sharpening the portfolio to Wrangler + Helly Hansen.
verified.dated_milestone: Regulatory close expected 2H26 (next: HSR/regulatory clearance; ongoing $750M buyback execution); $0.53 dividend record date Jun 8, 2026
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Authentic Brands Group, signed definitive agreement) + G2 (concrete commitment: up to $1B / $750M cash + $250M earnout, proceeds to $750M buyback + term-loan paydown)
verified.edge: L — both the planned divestiture+$750M buyback (May 7) and the signed definitive agreement (May 21) are already public and ~2-4 weeks digested; stock at $70.07 sits on its 50d ($70.81)/200d ($71.55) averages and ~20% below the $87 52wk high, so the announcement effect has faded and no undisclosed surprise figure (G3) remains.
verified.primary_source: Kontoor Brands press release "Enters Into Definitive Agreement to Sell Lee Business to Authentic Brands Group" via Business Wire, May 21, 2026 (corroborated by Authentic Brands PRNewswire same day; planned divestiture + $750M buyback first disclosed in Q1 results May 7, 2026)
verified.analysis: Catalyst is genuine, dated and specific — a named-counterparty definitive sale of Lee to Authentic Brands for up to $1B against a $3.87B market cap, paired with a $750M buyback and deleveraging, with a 2H26 regulatory close. But the binding event already fired on May 21 (and the divestiture+buyback were pre-announced May 7), so the primary re-rate is behind us; the remaining forward step is a routine, market-expected regulatory close rather than a fresh unpriced surprise. With the stock mid-range/below highs and no G3 unpriced figure disclosed, this is a known capital-return reshape with low informational edge — WATCH for confirmed close/buyback pace, not a high-conviction fresh ACTIVE catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Live, shareholder-approved merger arb: Kimberly-Clark is acquiring Kenvue for $3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB shares/share (~$17.99 implied at KMB $99.04), with remaining foreign regulatory clearances gating a targeted 2H26 close.
verified.dated_milestone: EC Foreign Subsidies Regulation provisional decision deadline June 8, 2026; China SAMR review ongoing (disclosed May 29, 2026); targeted close 2H26.
verified.instrument: stock (long KVUE arb; properly hedged short ~0.14625 KMB/share to isolate the ~1.6% spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Kimberly-Clark / KMB) + G2 (concrete binding merger agreement + dated EC FSR June-8 deadline & China SAMR review)
verified.edge: L — gross spread only ~$0.28 / ~1.6% ($17.99 implied vs $17.71) to a 2H26 close; ~80% of the consideration is the KMB stock leg, so a naked long is mostly acquirer-equity beta, not idiosyncratic alpha. Today's +4.9% KVUE pop was mechanical (KMB +4.8% lifting the stock leg), not a deal re-rate. The two hard catalysts (shareholder vote Jan-29, HSR clearance Feb-4) already fired; residual risk skews to downside (China SAMR remedies).
verified.primary_source: Kenvue/KMB Form 425 & 8-K (shareholder approval Jan-29-2026, HSR expiry Feb-4-2026); MLex "Kimberly-Clark seeks EU FSR approval" (EC provisional deadline June-8-2026); GuruFocus (China SAMR review disclosed May-29-2026); FMP quotes KVUE $17.71 / KMB $99.04 (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: Catalyst is real and dated — binding KMB/Kenvue merger ($3.50 cash + 0.14625 KMB), shareholders approved Jan-29-2026, US HSR cleared Feb-4, with a forward EC FSR provisional deadline of June-8-2026 and China SAMR review pending into a 2H26 close. But it gates to WATCH not ACTIVE: the gross spread is thin (~1.6%) and an unhedged long KVUE is dominated by KMB-equity beta — today's +4.9% move was KMB dragging the stock leg, not a Kenvue-specific re-rating. As a properly hedged arb it offers a modest, low-edge ~1.6% to close with asymmetric SAMR downside; as a naked long it is effectively a bet on KMB, bordering on a macro/sector-beta position rather than clean catalyst alpha.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash go-private at $10.90/share (Kona Bidco: Fairfax + McMorrow/management consortium, $1.65B Fairfax funding) heads to a binding shareholder vote, but shares already trade ABOVE the deal price.
verified.dated_milestone: Special meeting / shareholder vote on June 10, 2026 (2/3 supermajority ex-affiliates); Q2-2026 expected close.
verified.instrument: stock (merger-arb) — but negative spread; only a topping-bid/bump call has upside, and none is sourced
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Fairfax/Kona Bidco) and G2 (concrete commitment: $10.90 cash, $1.65B Fairfax funding) PASS. G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure: no competing bid or announced bump, only shareholder-litigation "investigations" pressuring price.
verified.edge: L — at $11.01 vs $10.90 deal the arb spread is negative (~-1.0%; even with the $0.12 close dividend it nets ~flat). Upside requires an unconfirmed price bump; the deal economics are effectively re-rated.
verified.primary_source: DEFM14A merger proxy (special meeting set June 10, 2026), proxy dated May 5, 2026; Globe & Mail "Kennedy-Wilson Tightens Shareholder Approval Terms" (Mar 15, 2026 amendment); FMP quote $11.01 (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The merger itself is real, dated, and forward (vote June 10, not yet fired), clearing G1/G2 — but it is NOT a tradeable long because KW already prints $11.01 vs the $10.90 cash terms, a negative-carry spread. The "market sniffing a bump" framing is unsupported: I found no competing bidder or announced increase, only multiple law-firm fairness investigations, which historically seldom force a higher price and supply no specific unpriced figure (G3 fail). Net: genuine deal but the ACTIVE bump thesis is speculative, so this is WATCH — flip to ACTIVE only on a sourced topping bid or board-agreed price increase.

verified.catalyst: All-stock acquisition by Charter (0.236x CHTR/share, holders approved), accelerated to close contemporaneously with the Charter-Cox combination.
verified.dated_milestone: Operative date is now Charter-Cox regulatory: CA PUC decision urged by ~July 16, 2026; DOJ/HSR clearance expires Sep 15, 2026 (GCI spin already completed July 14, 2025).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Charter) and G2 (definitive, holder-approved merger) are met, but G3 fails — no specific currently-unpriced figure; spread is fully compressed.
verified.edge: L — at 0.236x, LBRDA deal value = $31.18 vs price $30.88 = ~1% gross spread over a 6-12mo+ horizon; residual is a directional CHTR-equity bet, not idiosyncratic arb edge.
verified.primary_source: Liberty Broadband PR "Provides Update Regarding Pending Acquisition by Charter" + GCI spin-off completion PR (July 14, 2025); Charter-Cox HSR/CA-PUC 8-K/425 filings (Jan 2026); FMP quotes LBRDA $30.88 / CHTR $132.12 (2026-06-05).
verified.analysis: Fired, fully-priced merger-arb: Charter buyout announced Nov-2024, holders approved, exchange ratio public, and the GCI spin gating cited in the flag is stale (completed July 14, 2025). At CHTR $132.12 the 0.236x ratio implies $31.18 vs LBRDA $30.88 — only ~1% to deal value, and the entire complex is down ~70% from 52-wk highs (CHTR $422 high), so the only live variable is Charter's own collapsing equity plus Charter-Cox regulatory timing (HSR expires Sep 15 2026, CA PUC pending) — a directional/macro bet, not an unpriced forward catalyst. Fails strict gate (fired + re-rated + no specific unpriced figure); the residual is Charter-equity beta, explicitly excluded.

verified.catalyst: Liberty Global is folding VodafoneZiggo + Telenet into a new "Ziggo Group" (close 2H-2026), then listing it on Euronext Amsterdam in 2027 and spinning 90% to LBTYK/LBTYA holders — a classic Malone SOTP value-unlock at a deep discount.
verified.dated_milestone: Ziggo Group CEO van Rooyen + CFO Fruytier start Sep 1, 2026; transaction close 2H-2026; Euronext Amsterdam listing + 90% spin in 2027. Next hard date: Sep 1, 2026 (mgmt installs).
verified.instrument: Equity (LBTYK common; LBTYA more liquid). Long-dated calls viable given multi-quarter, multi-leg unlock timeline into 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1+G2+G3. G1: named counterparty Vodafone (definitive agreement, €1.0bn cash + 10% Ziggo stake). G2: concrete board-level commitment — Ziggo Group formation, named CEO/CFO (Sep 1 start), 2027 Euronext listing + 90% spin. G3: specific unpriced gap — mgmt SOTP base case ~$27 vs $11.33 spot; ~$3.8bn mkt cap vs $11–15bn cited asset value.
verified.edge: H — structural, idiosyncratic value-unlock (not macro); deal signed Feb 2026 yet stock has NOT re-rated (LBTYK $11.33, below 50d/200d avgs, nearer year-low than high), so the close/listing/spin catalysts are all still forward. Edge tempered by execution risk (close not yet final, listing 12–18mo out) and high subsidiary leverage (4–5x EBITDA), but the dated, named, board-committed path is exactly the Sunrise spin-off playbook that historically narrowed the discount.
verified.primary_source: Liberty Global press release "Names Stephen van Rooyen as New Ziggo Group CEO Ahead of Planned Amsterdam Listing in 2027" (Jun 1, 2026) + originating BusinessWire/8-K deal announcement (Feb 18, 2026); SOTP/price context GuruFocus + Seeking Alpha (Jun 2026); spot $11.33 LBTYK (FMP quote, Jun 5, 2026).
verified.kill_fact: The deal structure is CONFIRMED by Liberty Global's primary Feb-18-2026 release: acquire Vodafone's 50% of VodafoneZiggo for EUR1.0bn cash + 10% of new 'Ziggo Group' (VodafoneZiggo+Telenet), close 2H-2026, list on Euronext Amsterdam 2027, spin 90% to LBTYK/LBTYA holders; van Rooyen named CEO; ~EUR1bn synergy NPV. The 2027 listing/spin leg is genuinely forward, so the catalyst is NOT fully fired. BUT the line's load-bearing thesis hook is FALSE: it claims the stock 'has not re-rated since announcement... entirely ahead rather than priced/fired.' LBTYA/LBTYK jumped ~16.76% on the Feb-18 announcement day — the catalyst's announcement leg already fired with a material re-rate. Current LBTYK $11.33 (mkt cap $3.83bn confirmed) sits at the 200-day avg ($11.47), well above the 52wk low $9.67, i.e. at the post-deal level, not a pre-announcement discount. Magnitude also overstated: the '~$27 SOTP' is management's aspirational base case, not the realistic mark — honest sell-side fair value clusters ~$15.92 (31% upside) with Hold targets at $11.50, and Ziggo Group alone is pegged at ~$12-16/share (~= current cap). So genuine remaining edge is the gap to ~$15-16 SOTP plus a ~18-month listing lag and high silo leverage, not an $11->$27 unlock. Real, dated, idiosyncratic but partially priced and story-heavy; 8 is too high.
verified.analysis: LBTYK clears all three Bloom gates with a real, dated, forward catalyst: a definitive Feb-2026 agreement with Vodafone creates Ziggo Group (VodafoneZiggo + Telenet, €6.6bn rev, 13m subs), set to close 2H-2026, list on Euronext Amsterdam in 2027, and spin 90% to Liberty holders — with the operating management team (van Rooyen/Fruytier) installed Sep 1, 2026. The stock trades at ~$3.8bn vs management's ~$27 SOTP base case and sell-side $11–15bn asset estimates, and has not re-rated since announcement, so the unlock is entirely ahead rather than priced/fired. Primary execution risks (close finalization, ~18-month listing lag, high silo leverage) keep this an 8, not a 9, but the named/dated/idiosyncratic structure mirrors the prior Sunrise spin that worked.

verified.catalyst: WHP Global's capped $100M tender for Lands' End shares at $45.00 (for a ~7% stake) has already closed — it expired March 31 and settled April 1, 2026 with shares accepted on a ~7.6% proration — so the partial-liquidity event is fired, not forward.
verified.dated_milestone: No forward catalyst tied to the tender; next dated event is the routine Q1 FY2026 earnings call on 2026-06-09 (fundamentals, not a Bloom-gate catalyst).
verified.bloom_gates: None pass on a FORWARD basis. The tender met G1 (named counterparty WHP) and G2 (concrete $100M/$45 commitment) but those are now in the past/fired and fully re-rated. No remaining G3 specific currently-unpriced figure.
verified.edge: L — event concluded and priced; stock at $10.66 vs the $45 tender print, no residual takeout optionality since the deal was explicitly capped at ~7% (a JV, not an acquisition).
verified.primary_source: SEC SC 14D9/A and SC TO-T/A via StockTitan/SEC EDGAR — tender expired 2026-03-31, closed 2026-04-01, 2,222,222 shares accepted at $45.00 (~7.6% proration); FMP quote LE $10.66 as of 2026-06-06.
verified.analysis: The triage thesis is a fired event: WHP Global's tender (SC TO-T / SC 14D9) closed April 1, 2026, buying the capped 2,222,222 shares (~7% stake) at $45.00 on a ~7.6% proration after ~95% of shares were tendered — the partial-liquidity opportunity is fully concluded and re-rated. Because this was a capped purchase forming a JV rather than a takeout, there is no follow-on squeeze-out or full-sale catalyst; the only forward item is routine Q1 FY2026 earnings on June 9, 2026. Fails the FORWARD/not-fired gate, so tier NONE.

verified.catalyst: Somnigroup (SGI) is acquiring Leggett & Platt in a signed all-stock deal at a fixed 0.1455 SGI-per-LEG ratio (~$2.5B), with LEG holders ending up ~9% of the combined company and close targeted by year-end 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: LEG 2/3 shareholder special-meeting vote (date not yet set; proxy/S-4 pending) + remaining foreign clearances (Canada/EU/UK/Korea/Austria FDI); expected close by year-end 2026. US HSR waiting period already expired 2026-06-03.
verified.instrument: stock (long LEG hedged short 0.1455 SGI — fixed-ratio stub swap, no cash leg)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Somnigroup) + G2 (concrete signed all-stock agreement, 0.1455 ratio). G3 FAILS: no specific currently-unpriced figure — LEG $10.01 already sits ~+1% above deal value 0.1455x SGI $68.01 = $9.90.
verified.edge: L — fixed-ratio stock swap, not a cash takeout; gross arb spread is flat-to-NEGATIVE (LEG trades ~1% through deal value), and the main US antitrust gate (HSR) already cleared 6/3/26 so the binary is largely priced. Residual return is a directional SGI/acquirer-stub bet, not clean capturable merger-arb alpha.
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire / SEC Form 425 deal announcement (signed 2026-04-13, 0.1455 ratio, ~9% combined-co ownership, close by YE2026); StockTitan/TradingView SEC 8-K HSR waiting period expired 2026-06-03; FMP live quotes LEG $10.01 / SGI $68.01 (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, named, and dated (signed Somnigroup all-stock acquisition, 0.1455 fixed exchange ratio, HSR cleared 6/3/26, close by year-end 2026), satisfying Bloom G1/G2. But it fails the edge/unpriced test: at LEG $10.01 vs SGI $68.01 the deal value is ~$9.90, so LEG already trades slightly THROUGH parity — there is no positive spread to harvest, and as a fixed-ratio stub swap (not cash) any P&L is effectively a bet on Somnigroup's own share price. With the largest US regulatory gate behind it and the spread re-rated to flat/negative, this is a stale, picked-clean arb rather than a forward-unpriced opportunity — WATCH only, pending a vote date or a spread that reopens.

verified.catalyst: Two legs: a Series A preference-share special dividend (already declared/record-dated, effectively priced) and a pending appeal of Costa Rica SUTEL's block of the Liberty/Millicom-Tigo JV — but the appeal has no announced ruling date and covers only the immaterial Costa Rica unit.
verified.dated_milestone: Special-div pay ~6/16/2026 (record 6/1 already passed = fired); SUTEL appeal (RCS-263-2025, filed 10/22/2025) verdict pending with NO announced date.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Millicom/Tigo on the appeal) — but fails the DATED test; special-div fails FORWARD (record date passed, priced).
verified.edge: L — special div is a known, already-record-dated capital-return mechanic (1x 9.0% perpetual pref per 10 commons) that is priced; the SUTEL appeal is a defensive, low-probability reversal (regulator deemed remedies insufficient), undated, and covers only the Costa Rica operation (~14% Millicom-side, immaterial to LILA group EV).
verified.primary_source: FMP/Business Wire "Liberty Latin America Announces Key Dates Regarding Special Dividend of Series A Preference Shares" (2026-06-01) and WebSearch on SUTEL RCS-263-2025 appeal (BNamericas / Developing Telecoms / Q Costa Rica, Nov 2025 — appeal filed 10/22/2025, verdict pending, no date as of 2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The $500M Series A preference special dividend is real and dated but already at/past its 6/1/2026 record date (declared 5/21, pays ~6/16), so it is fired and largely priced — not a forward catalyst. The Costa Rica SUTEL merger-block appeal is genuinely forward with a named counterparty (Millicom/Tigo) but has NO announced ruling date, is a low-probability reversal (SUTEL's RCS-263-2025 found proposed remedies insufficient), and concerns only the small Costa Rica JV — immaterial to LILA's group valuation. Neither leg clears the strict gate (priced/fired div + undated, low-edge, immaterial appeal), so NONE.

verified.catalyst: Board-run strategic review (BofA + Wachtell engaged) evaluating a potential sale of the entire company, running alongside a separate active process to sell the Specialty segment.
verified.dated_milestone: No deal milestone date set (company states "no deadline/timetable"); only firm near-term date is the 6/22/2026 securities class-action lead-plaintiff deadline, which is litigation, not an M&A milestone.
verified.instrument: Equity (event-driven). Long-dated calls only as a low-conviction lottery given binary, untimed outcome; size small.
verified.bloom_gates: None cleared strictly. G1 fails (no named buyer for company or Specialty). G2 fails (no binding agreement; board only "evaluating," explicit "no assurance," no timetable). G3 fails (no disclosed bid/valuation figure; synergy/class-period numbers are backward-looking litigation).
verified.edge: M (review is real and demonstrably NOT priced — stock $25.22 near 52wk low $23.98, ~18% below 50d MA, down ~26% — so optionality is live; but edge capped by absence of any named counterparty, committed figure, or dated deal milestone, plus a competing fraud-litigation overhang).
verified.primary_source: LKQ 8-K / GlobeNewswire "LKQ Initiates Strategic Review to Maximize Shareholder Value" (2026-01-26), BofA + Wachtell engaged, includes potential sale of the Company; status confirmed via FMP LKQ news through 2026-06-05 (no deal; only 6/22 class-action deadline) and FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($25.22, near 52wk low).
verified.analysis: The structural setup is genuine and specific: on 1/26/2026 LKQ's board launched a review explicitly including a whole-company sale with BofA Securities and Wachtell Lipton engaged, on top of a separate live Specialty-segment ($409M Q1 rev) sale process, and as of 6/5/2026 no deal, no named bidder, and no valuation has been disclosed. It clears none of the three Bloom gates strictly — no named counterparty (G1), no concrete commitment (G2), no specific unpriced figure (G3) — and the only hard date (6/22 lead-plaintiff) is a securities class action, not a deal milestone. It is forward and clearly un-rated (price near 52-wk lows), so it merits WATCH for a deal/segment-sale announcement rather than ACTIVE; trigger would be a signed agreement or named counterparty.

verified.catalyst: Board is evaluating a PE-backed Curium ~$7B takeover proposal for Lantheus, with a decision reportedly weeks away and the PYLARIFY franchise potentially drawing competing bidders.
verified.dated_milestone: Board decision on Curium offer reportedly expected by ~June 29, 2026; near-term watch = Goldman Sachs Healthcare Conf (Jun 3) commentary already passed, next is any 8-K/definitive-agreement filing.
verified.instrument: Forward merger-arb: long common at ~$101 vs ~$103 implied offer (~$7B), with upside optionality to a raised/competing bid; tight downside given strong standalone fundamentals.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Curium Pharma, Bloomberg 2026-05-22) and G3 (Truist calls $7B "light," implying unpriced upside + competing-bidder optionality on PYLARIFY). G2 partial — formal proposal received and board engaged, but no signed definitive agreement yet.
verified.edge: M — counterparty named and process live/forward, but stock has already re-rated hard (52w low $47 -> $101, 50d avg $86.8), so the base ~$7B bid is largely priced; remaining edge is the spread to a final/higher bid + competing-bidder optionality, not a fresh dislocation.
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg via Reuters/FiercePharma, "Lantheus weighs potential $7 billion sale after Curium's offer" (2026-05-22); decision-timing/"light" valuation corroborated by Sahm Capital/Truist (2026-05-26); live/forward confirmed via FMP LNTH news + quote $100.98 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: CORE CONFIRMED + FORWARD: Reuters (2026-05-22) confirms PE-backed Curium made a ~$7B takeover proposal and Lantheus board is evaluating a potential sale; no definitive agreement as of June 3-6 (news flow is Goldman/William Blair conferences + SNMMI Image-of-Year). Stock $100.98, yearHigh $104 (market cap $6.57B). Truist (2026-05-26) called $7B 'light,' citing $115-125/share as 'more palatable' takeout — corroborating live, unpriced bump/competing-bid optionality on the PYLARIFY/radiopharma franchise. CORRECTION (date error, not a kill): the '~June 29' decision deadline is WRONG — June 29, 2026 is the FDA PDUFA date for LNTH-2501 (Ga-68 edotreotide), per the 2026-03-17 GlobeNewsWire release, NOT an M&A decision date. Reports say a decision could be 'weeks away' but cite no specific date; the line conflated an unrelated regulatory catalyst with the takeover timeline. Minor: '~$103 implied' understates — $7B is ~$107-108/share. Thesis (real, dated, forward single-name merger-arb with genuine bump optionality) stands; edge capped Medium as headline offer is largely in the price.
verified.analysis: Real, specific, dated sale process: PE-backed Curium made a ~$7B takeover proposal (Bloomberg, May 22, 2026), the board is actively evaluating, and a decision is reportedly weeks away (~June 29); Truist flags the price as "light," supporting competing-bidder optionality on the PYLARIFY/radiopharma franchise. Confirmed forward and not fired — as of June 3-6 news flow is conference/clinical (Goldman Sachs, SNMMI award), with no definitive agreement or close announced and the stock at $100.98 vs ~$103 implied. Single-name event (not a macro bet); edge is capped at Medium because the headline offer is already substantially in the price, leaving the bump/competing-bid spread as the live, unpriced leg.

verified.catalyst: IM-3 Nova-C lunar landing attempt (Reiner Gamma) plus deployment of the first NSNS data-relay satellite, the third IM landing after IM-2 tipped over.
verified.dated_milestone: IM-3 launch only loosely scheduled "second half 2026" — no firm date confirmed (Wikipedia, as of Jan 7 2026); SpaceX IPO June 12 2026 is the nearer dated event driving the space-trade sentiment.
verified.bloom_gates: None cleanly met. G1 counterparty (NASA) exists but the binding $4.82B NSNS award was made in 2024 (already fired, not forward). G2/G3 fail: IM-3 has no concrete dated commitment and no specific currently-unpriced figure — outcome is operational landing success, not a value unlock.
verified.primary_source: Wikipedia IM-3 (schedule "2H 2026, no firm date," as of 2026-01-07); Motley Fool "Why Intuitive Machines Stock Crashed Today" (2026-06-03, $500M ATM raise); Benzinga "LUNR Craters After Losing NASA LTV Contract" (2026-05-26); FMP quote LUNR -12.7% (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: IM-3 is a real execution binary but lacks a firm date ("2H 2026" only) and is sentiment/operational, not a structural value unlock — the NSNS relay economics ($4.82B max, 10-yr) were already awarded in 2024 and known. LUNR has run +201.8% over 6 months and +73% in May on SpaceX-IPO space-trade momentum, so the catalyst is heavily re-rated/fired, while recent fundamentals turned negative: it LOST the NASA LTV contract (May 26) and launched a $500M ATM dilution offering (June 3), with the stock down -12.7% today on that dilution. verified.catalyst: Glazer family members are publicly studying a sale of their 51% control block (Bloomberg, Jun-3-2026), and any 100% sale closing before Feb-2027 must clear a contractual $33/share drag-along floor for Ratcliffe/INEOS — vs ~$22 spot.

verified.instrument: Equity (MANU common, NYSE); long-dated calls if pricing a pre-Feb-2027 deal toward the $33 floor.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Ratcliffe/INEOS locked into the mechanism) + G2 (concrete contractual $33/share drag-along floor in NYSE proxy filing); G3 partial — $33 floor is a specific figure but is downside support, not an on-the-table bid price.
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg, "Glazer Family Members Are Said to Study Man United Stake Sale," Jun-3-2026; drag-along/$33-floor terms per MANU NYSE proxy filing (Dec-2023 Ratcliffe deal), floor effective until Feb-2027.
verified.analysis: The $33/share drag-along floor is real and documented in MANU's NYSE proxy filing: it protects Ratcliffe's ~29% stake only if a full sale closes before February 2027, after which the Glazers can drag him at any price — a hard, dated structural cliff (G2 concrete commitment + named counterparty Ratcliffe/INEOS = G1). The Jun-3 Bloomberg report is genuinely fresh and forward, but it is exploratory ("family members studying," no unified decision, possibly only a fractional sale, ~£6bn ask vs ~$3.8bn market cap) with NO named buyer for the control block and NO signed transaction, so the M&A event itself is unpriced optionality, not a committed deal. Spot $21.95 (near 52wk high $22.94, year low $13.80) sits well below the $33 floor, framing real downside-anchored control-sale optionality into the Feb-2027 cliff, but absent a committed bid this is WATCH, not ACTIVE.
